scenario.summary: Q4 supplier contract renewal cycle — 12 contracts, spend analytics, vendor evaluation
scenario.situation: It's November, and 12 major supplier contracts are up for renewal by year-end. The Procurement Manager needs to evaluate each contract against spend analytics, vendor performance data, and market benchmarks. Spend analytics has flagged $2.3M in potential consolidation savings across overlapping categories. Three vendors — Summit Logistics, Pacific Fasteners, and Midwest Tooling — are underperforming on delivery metrics. Meanwhile, the raw material spike is creating urgency around renegotiating steel and alloy supply agreements with GlobalSteel and Rheinmetall.
dataInputs: 12 expiring supplier contracts with terms and pricing
3-year spend analytics by category and vendor
Market benchmark pricing for key categories
Purchase order history and volume trends
AP payment data and early-pay discount utilization
agentSteps: Analyzes $1.2B annual addressable spend across 12 expiring contracts — categorizes by strategic importance and renewal urgency
Runs spend consolidation analysis: identifies 3 categories where dual-sourcing can be consolidated to primary supplier — $2.3M projected savings
Generates vendor scorecards combining quality (PPM defect rates), delivery (on-time %), price competitiveness, and payment terms
Flags 3 underperforming vendors: Summit Logistics (82% on-time vs. 95% SLA), Pacific Fasteners (rising defect rate), Midwest Tooling (15% price premium vs. market)
Models renegotiation scenarios for GlobalSteel and Rheinmetall: volume commitment increases in exchange for price caps and extended terms
Calculates total cost of ownership (TCO) including logistics, quality costs, and payment term optimization — identifies $890K in hidden costs
Generates contract renewal recommendations with prioritized negotiation strategies and projected savings by vendor
scenario.expectedOutput: Contract renewal package: 12 vendor evaluations with scorecards, 3 consolidation opportunities ($2.3M), renegotiation strategies for top 5 suppliers, TCO analysis, and projected annual savings of $3.8M

contracts.recommendation: Renew — negotiate volume discount + price cap for Q1
Renew — extend to 2-year term for price stability
Renew with quality improvement clause — PPM trending up
Issue corrective action — 82% on-time vs. 95% SLA, consider backup carrier
Consolidate into KuboTech contract — $1.1M savings, better quality
Renegotiate — 15% price premium vs. market benchmark
Expand scope — absorb Pacific Fasteners volume for consolidation discount
Renew as-is — strong performance, competitive pricing
Renew — consider as Summit Logistics backup for Americas
Renew with price escalation cap tied to commodity index
Consolidate with Apex order for volume discount — $0.4M savings
Renew — add dual-source clause for supply chain resilience
